scene_summary: A former intern (Corbin) who's been at LARP for 16 months gets a full-time offer but is offered a platform engineering role by Verso Jobs instead; he declines to stay and leaves.

observations.likely_role: Verso Jobs recruiter / platform engineering hiring manager
signature_lines: Yo, Corbin Braxton, what up, bro? I'm chilling man.
So you'd rather play theater with a bunch of corporate muppets and build something with your body that could actually change the world
We got to fill this role fast. So if you change your mind, I can't promise we'll have anything for you.
observations.interpersonal_note: Professional but pushy; keeps trying to convince Corbin to take the job despite his hesitation.

signature_lines: Yo, Corbin Braxton, what up, bro? I'm chilling man.
Yeah logistics analytics and reporting partners.
So you'd rather play theater with a bunch of corporate muppets and build something with your body that could actually change the world
I literally just got to sit in these meetings I pull up gpt and it gives me some linkedin speak jargon to spit out and these guys eat it up every time
It's cheese. No offense, dude, but that sounds like hell.
Low-key like I feel like just sticking it out here might be the move long term
observations.interpersonal_note: Casual, laid-back; has been at LARP for 16 months as an intern and is now considering a full-time offer elsewhere

signature_lines: It's cheese. No offense, dude, but that sounds like hell.
Well, maybe but I mean it worked for my dad, right?
What's he making? Uh, never mind.
observations.interpersonal_note: Dismissive of Corbin's concerns; references his father as a cautionary example of corporate success
